specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, RMBO banding station, Homes Grove
description: Size - "medium to small", sparrow-sized. Not as large as a White-crowned Sparrow in-the-hand, larger than a Pine Siskin or American Goldfinch.  Shape - passerine, sparrow-finch   Bill - "finch-like" seedeater bill, conical. Mottled in color - mostly dark brown-black areas and flesh-orange. Darker, overall upper mandible, but not solidly colored. Perhaps still showing remnant gape coloring.  Head - evenly light green-olive in color. No stripes, spots, or obvious differences in lores, nape, auriculars, etc.  Eye ring - somewhat wide, even, complete, pale yellow-tan colored  Body - Nape. mantle, and rump all even green-olive, with some bluish tinges. Sides,flanks - more yellow-olive Belly, vent - more buffy-yellow Overall - dark green-olive above, buff-yellow underneath   Wings - overall primaries, secondaries, coverts dark, brown-gray with slight yellow-olive edging. Alula evenly dark with light edging, Lesser coverts with more blue-green with olive edging. Wing Bars pretty indistinct.  Tail - also dark base color with olive edging, possible with growth bars  Skull - skulling (by a novice) showed incomplete ossification Legs-feet - bluish-black, perching foot,Behaviors: None observed. Bird was captured in bottom trammel of a mist net, and was on the ground. Upon release, after banding, it vacated immediately. Bird was calm in-the-hand and alert.Call: nonePlumage: hatch year
photographer: Photographer: Steve Brown, Steven Brown, Steve Brown, Steve Brown, Steve Brown
